analysis.bounded_question: What is the smallest honest Ratchet-native architecture and fitness guidance slice we can keep without importing generic review authority or migration workflow?

candidate_scope.focus_candidate: current Ratchet imported-cluster and graph-support evolution
candidate_scope.title: bounded lev architecture and fitness audit

proposed_fitness_functions: graph truth remains equal active/graphed with zero missing and zero stale
no migration, patch, ADR/C4 broadness, or imported runtime flags in the packet
analysis.recommended_follow_on_shape: only a later bounded candidate-specific fitness-function or scenario probe if this audit remains clean
review_triggers: candidate widens into migration or runtime ownership
graph truth diverges from registry truth
a new unopened lev cluster is later added to the selector
sensitivity_points: generic architecture-review import becomes authority creep
migration or patch execution turns analysis support into runtime ownership
analysis.smallest_honest_slice: one bounded repo-held audit over quality attributes, tradeoffs, bounded fitness-function proposals, and explicit forbid lines
tradeoff_points: higher structure and explicit tradeoffs vs less generic review breadth
bounded fitness-function proposals vs any implied automation or governance ownership

non_goal_hygiene.evidence: candidate request does not widen into generic review authority, migration, or runtime ownership

adapt_away_from: full ADR generation as the first slice
full C4 generation as the first slice

non_goals: No full ADR or C4 generation in the first slice.
No PR verdict authority such as APPROVE or REQUEST_CHANGES.
No migration, patch application, registry update, or commit ownership.
No imported runtime, builder workflow, or Leviathan path ownership.
No claim that Ratchet now has a live architecture-governance runtime.
recommended_actions: Keep this slice audit-only, repo-held, and nonoperative.
Use it to scope one later candidate-specific fitness-function or scenario probe only if the gate stays clean.
Do not widen this slice into generic review authority, full ADR/C4 output, migration, patching, or imported runtime ownership.

adr_or_c4_artifact_generation.why: full ADR/C4 artifact generation is too broad for the first Ratchet-native slice

candidate_option_set.why: the first Ratchet slice should compare bounded options rather than collapsing directly to one imported recommendation

fitness_function_proposals.why: fitness checks may be proposed as future bounded probes, not imported as live governance or CI ownership

migration_or_patch_execution.why: lev-builder execution paths are out of scope for the first architecture/fitness slice

quality_attribute_elicitation.why: the strongest reusable `arch` value is forcing explicit architectural drivers instead of vague review language

review_trigger_thresholds.why: thresholds are useful as bounded stop/revisit markers, not as external review cadence import

tradeoff_analysis.why: tradeoffs are the safe imported core; they sharpen guidance without creating runtime authority
